summary.text: Extension methods for configuring EF Core migration resources.

members.name: PublishAsMigrationScript
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<EFMigrationResource> EFMigrationResourceBuilderExtensions.PublishAsMigrationScript(this IResourceBuilder<EFMigrationResource> builder, bool idempotent = true, bool noTransactions = false)

summary.text: Configures the EF migration resource to generate a migration script during publishing.

remarks.text: , the generated SQL script is written to the publish output directory under the
folder. The script is included as a deployment artifact, but it is not executed automatically during deployment.

idempotent.text: (the default), generates an idempotent script with
checks so it can be safely re-run against a database that has already had some or all of the migrations applied.

noTransactions.text: , omits transaction statements from the script.

summary.text: Configures the EF migration resource to run database update when the AppHost starts.

children.text: When enabled, migrations are applied during AppHost startup. This only affects local run-mode execution. The migrations resource is not deployed with the app, so this method has no effect during publish or deployment.
A health check is automatically registered for this resource, allowing other resources to use
to wait until migrations complete before starting.

members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<EFMigrationResource> EFMigrationResourceBuilderExtensions.WithMigrationNamespace(this IResourceBuilder<EFMigrationResource> builder, string @namespace)

summary.text: Configures the namespace for new migrations created with the Add Migration command.

remarks.text: If not specified, the namespace will be derived from the project's default namespace. Example:

members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<EFMigrationResource> EFMigrationResourceBuilderExtensions.WithMigrationOutputDirectory(this IResourceBuilder<EFMigrationResource> builder, string outputDirectory)

summary.text: Configures the output directory for new migrations created with the Add Migration command.

remarks.text: If not specified, migrations will be placed in the default

outputDirectory.text: The output directory path relative to the project root.

members.name: WithMigrationsProject
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<EFMigrationResource> EFMigrationResourceBuilderExtensions.WithMigrationsProject(this IResourceBuilder<EFMigrationResource> builder, string projectPath)

summary.text: Configures a separate project containing the migrations using a project path.

children.text: Use this method when the migrations are in a different project than the startup project. The target project's path will be used for migration operations while the startup project remains the original project. The project resource on which AddEFMigrations is invoked should be the startup project (the project that contains the DbContext configuration).

projectPath.text: The path to the project file containing the migrations.

summary.text: Configures a separate project containing the migrations using a project metadata type.

children.text: Use this method when the migrations are in a different project than the startup project. The target project's path will be used for migration operations while the startup project remains the original project. The project resource on which AddEFMigrations is invoked should be the startup project (the project that contains the DbContext configuration).

TProject.text: The project metadata type generated by the Aspire build tooling.

summary.text: Provides extension methods for adding EF Core migration management to projects.

members.name: AddEFMigrations
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<EFMigrationResource> EFResourceBuilderExtensions.AddEFMigrations(this IResourceBuilder<ProjectResource> builder, string name, string dbContextTypeName)

summary.text: Adds EF Core migration management for a specific DbContext type.

remarks.text: Multiple calls to this method with different context types are supported, allowing you to manage migrations for multiple DbContexts in the same project.

returns.text: An EF migration resource builder for chaining additional configuration.

builder.text: The resource builder for the project.

dbContextTypeName.text: The fully qualified name of the DbContext type to manage migrations for.

name.text: The name of the migration resource.

description.text: Thrown if migrations for this context type have already been added.

members.name: AddEFMigrations
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<EFMigrationResource> EFResourceBuilderExtensions.AddEFMigrations(this IResourceBuilder<ProjectResource> builder, string name, string dbContextTypeName, Action<IResourceBuilder<DotnetToolResource>>? configureToolResource)

summary.text: Adds EF Core migration management for a specific DbContext type.

remarks.text: Multiple calls to this method with different context types are supported, allowing you to manage migrations for multiple DbContexts in the same project.

returns.text: An EF migration resource builder for chaining additional configuration.

builder.text: The resource builder for the project.

configureToolResource.text: Optional callback to configure the dotnet-ef tool resource used for migrations.

dbContextTypeName.text: The fully qualified name of the DbContext type to manage migrations for.

name.text: The name of the migration resource.

description.text: Thrown if migrations for this context type have already been added.

members.name: AddEFMigrations
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<EFMigrationResource> EFResourceBuilderExtensions.AddEFMigrations(this IResourceBuilder<ProjectResource> builder, string name)

summary.text: Adds EF Core migration management for the only DbContext type in the target project.

returns.text: An EF migration resource builder for chaining additional configuration.

builder.text: The resource builder for the project.

name.text: The name of the migration resource.

description.text: Thrown if migrations have already been added for any DbContext type on this project.

description.text: Thrown if migrations have already been added for any DbContext type on this project.

remarks.text: so it can be published as a container image that runs the migration bundle at deploy time when

members.name: .ctor
members.signature: public EFMigrationResource.EFMigrationResource(string name, ProjectResource projectResource, string? dbContextTypeName)

summary.text: Represents an EF Core migration resource associated with a project.

remarks.text: so it can be published as a container image that runs the migration bundle at deploy time when

dbContextTypeName.text: The fully qualified name of the DbContext type, or null to auto-detect.

projectResource.text: The parent project resource that contains the DbContext.

summary.text: Gets or sets the base container image for the migration bundle container.

remarks.text: When set, this overrides the default base image selection entirely. Use this when the default image (derived from the project's target framework) isn't suitable — for example when targeting a preview SDK or a custom base image with extra dependencies. Example:

summary.text: Gets or sets whether the migration bundle should be self-contained.
Gets or sets the target runtime identifier for the migration bundle (e.g., "linux-x64", "win-x64").
Gets the fully qualified name of the DbContext type to use for migrations, or null to auto-detect.

remarks.text: This property is used to specify which DbContext to use when the project contains multiple DbContext types. When null, the EF Core tools will auto-detect the DbContext to use.

summary.text: Gets or sets the namespace for new migrations. Used by the Add Migration command.

remarks.text: If not specified, the namespace will be derived from the project's default namespace.

summary.text: Gets or sets the output directory for new migrations. Used by the Add Migration command.

remarks.text: If not specified, migrations will be placed in the default 'Migrations' directory.

summary.text: Gets or sets the path to the project containing the migrations, when it's not the same as the startup project.

remarks.text: If not specified, migrations are assumed to be in the startup project. When specified, this project's path will be used as the target for migration operations.

summary.text: Gets the parent project resource that contains the DbContext.
Gets or sets whether a migration bundle should be generated during publishing.
Gets or sets whether a migration script should be generated during publishing.
Gets or sets whether the migration bundle should be published as a container image that applies the migrations to the database at deploy time.

remarks.text: , the resource is materialized as a

that wraps the migration bundle. The compute environment (Docker Compose, Azure Container Apps, Kubernetes, Azure App Service, Azure Functions, etc.) deploys it alongside the other compute resources and supplies the connection string from any

summary.text: Gets or sets whether the migration script should be idempotent (include IF NOT EXISTS checks).
Gets or sets whether to omit transaction statements from the migration script.
